artifact_only_materialization_acceptance: Every source artifact referenced by the future materialization phase matches the sha256 recorded in purg04_proof_loop_corpus_materialization_source_hashes.json.
Every future artifact binds explicitly to IF09-FIND-001, accepted_merged_track_a, patch commit 1e9a04a02846f3261ae72d0c95fbee6b0163b45b, and merge commit 7883af5a32c629026bfc6dc15ebee4ebbcadd295.

The RED->RESET->GREEN artifact states clearly that RED comes from the documentary baseline, reset comes from the cleanroom admissibility chain, and GREEN comes from already-recorded focused validations plus CI evidence.
The reset artifact states pre-reset state, reset attestation, and post-reset state from the accepted cleanroom lineage without implying a new reset was executed now.
The benign-flow artifact maps expected behavior from the approved bounded surface and observed behavior from already-recorded focused validations.
The kill-switch artifact is emitted only as an explicit not-applicable proof tied to IF06 policy plus the accepted Track A non-runtime boundary.
The rollback artifact maps merge-commit rollback path, merge parents, evidence-preservation rules, and the benign-after-rollback not-applicable reason from recorded sources only.
No future artifact created in the materialization phase claims new execution, Project_ARIS mutation, finding closure, remediation proof, product readiness, or Bedrock readiness.
The future materialization phase keeps can_open_purg05_now false and leaves ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json terminal with next_phase null.
rejection_conditions: Any future materialization step depends on a fresh Project_ARIS test run.
Any future materialization step depends on a Project_ARIS mutation or revert.
Any future materialization step cannot be derived from the recorded source hashes.
Any future materialization step tries to convert planning-only IF06 doctrine into a claim that a runtime kill-switch was exercised.
Any future materialization step tries to use the failed local remediation apply result as accepted-lineage proof instead of historical custody support.
